methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Primoco UAV is a publicly listed Defense & Security company founded by Ladislav Semetkovský. The valuation universe comprises 19 same-sector peers, of which 2 are public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 are private. Top private comparables include Evolv Technology, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. A rigorous same-stage and adjacent-stage filter yielded fewer than three datapoints, falling short of the minimum threshold required for statistical reliability. An all-sector median approach would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security subsegments. The company's €44M+ orderbook provides evidence of commercial traction but does not directly anchor a valuation multiple absent comparable revenue or profitability metrics.

Given the sparse narrow peer set, conventional triangulation methods cannot be reliably applied to Primoco UAV at this time. A defensible valuation range would require either (1) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent defense sectors, (2) direct revenue or EBITDA multiples from the two public peers if their financials materially resemble Primoco's profile, or (3) transaction-based or DCF analysis using the orderbook as a starting point. The primary uncertainty remains the lack of sufficient same-stage, same-subsegment comparables to establish median-based benchmarks.
